menu.no_games: No games played yet. Play your first game!
tutorial.welcome: Welcome to the interactive tutorial. You'll learn by practising each mechanic on prepared situations. I'll tell you what to do, you do it, I confirm. The tutorial has four phases: the basics, capturing, bringing home, then free play. Press Escape at any time to quit. Press Space to start.
tutorial.goal: The goal: bring all four of your pieces home before your opponent. You play Red, the computer plays Yellow. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.basics_intro: Phase 1 of 4: the basics. You'll learn to roll the dice, select a piece, and apply a die. Press Space to start.
tutorial.do_roll: Each turn you roll two dice before playing. To roll, press Space.
tutorial.do_select_for_exit: You rolled five and three. To bring a piece out of the base you need a five, on a single die or as a sum — that's the classic rule. Before applying a die, you must first select the piece that will play it. Press the one key to select your piece one.
tutorial.do_apply_a_for_exit: Piece one selected. Now to apply the first die — the five — press A. The A key is for the first die, Z for the second, S for the sum of both. Your piece will leave the base and arrive on its starting square.
tutorial.do_select_again: Well done, your piece one is on the board. You still have a three to play. Small rule: the selection resets after each move, so you must re-select. Press the one key.
tutorial.do_apply_z: Now to apply the second die — the three — press Z. Your piece will move three squares forward.
tutorial.end_of_basics: First phase complete. You can roll the dice, select a piece, and apply a die. Press Space to move on to capturing.
tutorial.capture_intro: Phase 2 of 4: capturing. When your piece lands on an opponent that's not on a safe square, you capture them: they go back to the base and you earn twenty bonus squares. I've set up a situation: your piece one is on its starting square, a Yellow piece is five squares ahead. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.capture_do_roll: Roll the dice by pressing Space.
tutorial.capture_do_select: You rolled five and one. The five lands exactly on the Yellow's square. Select your piece one by pressing one.
tutorial.capture_do_apply: Apply the five with the A key to capture the Yellow.
tutorial.capture_do_select_again: Capture successful. You earned twenty bonus squares, applied with the B key. As after each move, the selection has reset — re-select your piece one by pressing one.
tutorial.capture_do_bonus: Now press B to apply those twenty bonus squares. Your piece will move twenty more squares.
tutorial.end_of_capture: Second phase complete. You can capture and apply a bonus. Press Space to move on to bringing home.
tutorial.home_intro: Phase 3 of 4: bringing a piece home. To bring a piece home, you must apply the right die to land exactly on it. When a piece reaches home, you earn ten bonus squares. I've set up a situation: your piece one is in the final lane, one square from home. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.home_do_roll: Roll the dice by pressing Space.
tutorial.home_do_select: You rolled one and two. The one lets you land exactly on home. Select your piece one by pressing one.
tutorial.home_do_apply: Apply the one with the A key to bring your piece home.
tutorial.end_of_home: Third phase complete. You can bring a piece home. Press Space to hear a few advanced mechanics before the free phase.
tutorial.advanced_doubles: Advanced mechanic: doubles. If you roll a double — two identical dice — you play, then you play again a full new turn. But beware: three doubles in a row send your most advanced piece back to the base — that's the triple-double penalty. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.advanced_safe: Advanced mechanic: safe squares. On the ring, twelve squares out of sixty-eight are safe: no opponent can capture you there. When you navigate the board with arrow keys, a transition sound tells you when you enter or leave a safe zone. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.advanced_preview: Advanced mechanic: preview. Before applying a die, you can preview where your piece would land: Shift plus A, Shift plus Z, or Shift plus S after selecting a piece. It's risk-free: you hear the possible outcome without actually playing. Press Space to continue.
tutorial.free_play_intro: Phase 4 of 4: your turn. I've started a fresh game against the Yellow computer. Play freely as long as you like: Space to roll the dice, one to four to select a piece, A, Z and S to apply. And you have on-demand help keys: D for the dice, L for the board, I for selected piece details, H for the full help. When you're ready to finish the tutorial, press F. Good luck!
tutorial.wrap_up: Tutorial complete. You've practised the basics, capturing, bringing home and a real turn. You're ready for your first real game. My advice: start with Solo against one computer on Easy. Press Space or Escape to return to the menu.
tutorial.nudge_explain_select: Keys one to four select a piece
tutorial.nudge_explain_apply_s: S applies the sum of both dice
tutorial.nudge_explain_bonus: B applies the bonus squares from a capture or home arrival
tutorial.nudge_explain_finish: F ends the free play phase of the tutorial

help.full: Actions: Space rolls the dice. Keys 1 to 4 select a piece and read its position. A applies die 1, Z applies die 2, S applies the sum, B applies the bonus. T ends the turn. F2 pauses and resumes the game. F3 asks how much time is left if the timer is on. Per-die previews: Shift+A, Shift+Z, Shift+S, Shift+B tell you where that die would land you. Hints: C for the AI's recommended move. On demand: D for the current dice, I for the full piece summary, L for the board, J for the opponents, P for the journal, R to repeat the last announcement, K for statistics, M for achievements, H for this help. Navigation: arrow keys on the board, Tab between zones.
no_legal.in_base: {0} piece(s) in the base (a 5 is needed to come out)
crash.title: Error

xaml.customize_hint: Leave the fields empty to use the defaults. Only players present in the chosen mode are shown.
xaml.personality_aggressive: The Aggressor — captures at all costs, takes risks
xaml.personality_prudent: The Cautious — defensive, sticks to safe squares
xaml.personality_coureur: The Runner — races home, ignores captures
xaml.rules_text:
Goal: bring all 4 of your pieces home.

Each turn, you roll 2 dice (Space), choose a piece (1-4), and apply a die (A for the first, Z for the second, S for the sum).

To bring a piece out of the base, you need a 5 (on a die or as a sum).

If you roll a double, you play again. Three doubles in a row and your most advanced piece returns to the base.

If you land on an opponent (except on a safe square), you capture them and earn 20 bonus squares to apply with B.

When a piece reaches home, you earn 10 bonus squares.
xaml.board_automation: Game board — use arrow keys to navigate squares, Tab to leave for other zones
xaml.actions_hint: Choose a piece with 1-4. Apply with A (die 1), Z (die 2), S (sum), B (bonus).
xaml.journal_automation: Game journal — accessible with Tab only
xaml.settings_ambmusic_label: Ambient music volume (Chopin in menu, Satie in game)
xaml.settings_walkspeed_label: Per-square tap speed when a piece moves
xaml.settings_walkspeed_slow: Slow (a piece takes a long time to cross)
xaml.settings_timer_behavior_autoplay: Auto-play: the computer plays for you (recommended)
xaml.settings_timer_behavior_skip: Skip turn: you lose your turn
xaml.settings_timer_hint: In game: F2 pauses, F3 asks the time remaining. Audio warnings trigger at half-time, 10 seconds, and 5 seconds.
xaml.settings_legal_preview: Sound preview on selection (3 bell/tick tones indicating whether A, Z, and S are playable)
xaml.settings_opportunity_hints: Announce opportunities on roll ("capture possible", "home possible")
xaml.settings_zone_transition: Transition sounds when changing zone with arrow keys
xaml.settings_edge_bump: Error sound when bumping the edge of the board
xaml.settings_verbose: Long announcements (discovery mode) instead of concise mode
xaml.settings_immersive: Victorian parlor ambience (immersion phrases at start/end of game; coming soon: soft music and fireplace sounds)
xaml.endpanel_what_now: What would you like to do?
